title: Mass diarrhea dehydration station
summary: Focused extreme medical support for mass diarrhea dehydration station in offline, delayed-help, or collapse conditions.
content: When many people have diarrhea or vomiting, the survival task is clean water, separate toilets, hand hygiene, fluid access, and red-flag referral. This is not medical advice; it is a safety and organization checklist until qualified help is available.
materials: clean water if available. Context: “Mass diarrhea dehydration station”.
soap or sanitizer. Context: “Mass diarrhea dehydration station”.
gloves or clean barrier. Context: “Mass diarrhea dehydration station”.
paper and pen. Context: “Mass diarrhea dehydration station”.
phone, radio, or messenger — for «Mass diarrhea dehydration station»
labels for time and symptoms — for «Mass diarrhea dehydration station»
steps: Separate sick toilet area from kitchen and water storage.
Use safe water and official oral rehydration packets/instructions when available.
Track urine, drinking ability, vomiting, blood in stool, fever, and alertness.
Prioritize infants, elders, pregnancy, and chronic illness.
Clean hands and surfaces after every toilet or cleanup task.
warnings: This is not medical advice and it is not a doctor's advice. It is offline emergency organization guidance only. Use local emergency numbers, trained first aid, pharmacists, clinics, doctors, poison centers, and official public-health instructions whenever available. Do not perform surgery, invasive procedures, diagnosis, prescription changes, or medicine dosing from this guide. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Mass diarrhea dehydration station”.

Escalate immediately for trouble breathing, heavy bleeding, unconsciousness, severe pain, chest pain, stroke signs, severe burns, pregnancy danger signs, infants in distress, poisoning, or rapid worsening. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Mass diarrhea dehydration station”.
Do not delay evacuation or professional care to finish a checklist. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Mass diarrhea dehydration station”.
Keep privacy, consent, dignity, and local law in mind even during collapse. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Mass diarrhea dehydration station”.

The helper cannot keep the scene clean or safe during “Mass diarrhea dehydration station”

title: Shelter outbreak isolation lanes
summary: Focused extreme medical support for shelter outbreak isolation lanes in offline, delayed-help, or collapse conditions.
content: A crowded shelter needs clean lanes for healthy, exposed, sick, and recovering people to slow spread without abandoning care. This is not medical advice; it is a safety and organization checklist until qualified help is available.
materials: clean water if available. Context: “Shelter outbreak isolation lanes”.
soap or sanitizer. Context: “Shelter outbreak isolation lanes”.
gloves or clean barrier. Context: “Shelter outbreak isolation lanes”.
paper and pen. Context: “Shelter outbreak isolation lanes”.
phone, radio, or messenger — for «Shelter outbreak isolation lanes»
labels for time and symptoms — for «Shelter outbreak isolation lanes»
steps: Create separate sleeping and toilet zones if space allows.
Assign dedicated helpers for sick zones when possible.
Keep masks, ventilation, hand hygiene, and waste control visible.
Do not mix food service with sick care.
Post clear rules in both languages.

title: Fever with rash danger watch
summary: Focused extreme medical support for fever with rash danger watch in offline, delayed-help, or collapse conditions.
content: Fever with rash can be harmless or dangerous; in collapse, isolate gently and escalate red flags instead of guessing. This is not medical advice; it is a safety and organization checklist until qualified help is available.
materials: clean water if available. Context: “Fever with rash danger watch”.
soap or sanitizer. Context: “Fever with rash danger watch”.
gloves or clean barrier. Context: “Fever with rash danger watch”.
paper and pen. Context: “Fever with rash danger watch”.
phone, radio, or messenger — for «Fever with rash danger watch»
labels for time and symptoms — for «Fever with rash danger watch»
steps: Keep the person away from crowded sleeping and food zones.
Record fever start, rash appearance, travel, contacts, medicines, and vaccination history if known.
Escalate urgently for stiff neck, confusion, purple rash, breathing trouble, severe headache, pregnancy, infant, immune weakness, or rapid worsening.
Use hygiene barriers and ventilation.
Do not scrape or apply random chemicals.

title: Quarantine ethics and care minimums
summary: Focused extreme medical support for quarantine ethics and care minimums in offline, delayed-help, or collapse conditions.
content: Isolation without care becomes punishment. Even in outbreak collapse, people need water, food, sanitation, communication, and dignity. This is not medical advice; it is a safety and organization checklist until qualified help is available.
materials: clean water if available. Context: “Quarantine ethics and care minimums”.
soap or sanitizer. Context: “Quarantine ethics and care minimums”.
gloves or clean barrier. Context: “Quarantine ethics and care minimums”.
paper and pen. Context: “Quarantine ethics and care minimums”.
phone, radio, or messenger — for «Quarantine ethics and care minimums»
labels for time and symptoms — for «Quarantine ethics and care minimums»
steps: Explain the reason and expected review time.
Provide safe water, food, toilet access, medicine support, and a way to ask for help.
Keep families informed without exposing private details.
Review isolation daily and end it when official/local health guidance allows.
Do not use quarantine to punish enemies or unwanted people.
